title: Illustrative Unified Analysis determinism calibration
researchQuestion: Does repeated Unified Analysis of the same frozen input produce structurally identical findings and metrics under a controlled deterministic environment?

hypothesis.statement: Ten repeated Unified Analysis executions produce structurally identical ordered findings and metrics for the same frozen input and environment.
hypothesis.nullHypothesis: At least one of ten repeated Unified Analysis executions differs in ordered findings or metrics.
hypothesis.rationale: Deterministic ordering is expected to make identical controlled executions structurally repeatable; this record is illustrative and not evidence of execution.

motivation: Calibrate reproducible execution records against the QAIP 0.6 RC1 unified-analysis pipeline.

included: Validation and Coverage findings and metrics from Unified Analysis

independent.name: Execution repetition
independent.definition: Invocation number from one through ten

dependent.name: Structural output identity
dependent.definition: Exact equality of normalized ordered findings and metrics

controlled.name: Input and implementation
controlled.definition: Frozen QA Model input and repository commit

assumptions.statement: Canonical serialization removes irrelevant formatting differences.
assumptions.violationDetection: A semantic-equivalence review identifies a formatting-only digest difference.

requiredEvidence.description: Canonical output and checksum for every repetition
requiredEvidence.retention: Commit small illustrative outputs or retain in the controlled artifact store for one year

requiredEvidence.description: Command, exit code, stdout, and stderr for every repetition
requiredEvidence.retention: Retain with raw outputs for one year

supported.description: All ten output digests are equal.
supported.rationale: One unique digest represents structural identity across repetitions.

notSupported.description: More than one output digest is observed without a protocol deviation.
notSupported.rationale: Multiple digests contradict the frozen hypothesis under controlled conditions.

inconclusive.description: Fewer than ten valid outputs are available or a material deviation occurred.
inconclusive.reviewProcedure: Confirm run completeness and assess recorded deviations against the frozen protocol.
inconclusive.rationale: Incomplete or materially deviating evidence cannot resolve the hypothesis.

metricDefinitions.name: Valid completed runs
metricDefinitions.formula: count(runs with exit code 0 and complete output)

threatsToValidity.description: Canonicalization may omit meaningful output fields.
threatsToValidity.mitigation: Review the canonicalization field list before freeze.
threatsToValidity.residualRisk: Unmodeled semantic differences remain outside the conclusion.

considerations.security: Inputs and logs are checked for secrets before retention.
considerations.confidentiality: Only repository-owned frozen test data is used.

reproducibility.deterministicOrdering: Preserve engine order and documented finding tie-breakers.

randomness.absenceReason: The pipeline and comparison use no randomized operation.

reproducibility.timeouts: Five minutes per repetition; a timeout is a failed run.
reproducibility.retryPolicy: No replacement of failed runs; one diagnostic retry is retained separately.
reproducibility.secretsHandling: No secrets are required; scan retained logs before publication.
reproducibility.metricTolerancePolicy: All count and digest comparisons are exact.

changeHistory.change: Illustrative contract frozen for schema validation.
changeHistory.freezeImpact: Initial freeze; no execution is claimed.
